original text:
The Shedim are not afraid us.
There power short Adonai which can hold them.
The Utukku are safe behind the veil.
Their Kings celebrate our suffering the hidden lands.
The Rabisu know they can feed upon and live forever.
The Jinn know their power and revel it.
dusty rocks? The littlest sound, is. Like tinkle bell.
convey more fear our hearts?
The Shedim are made smoke and fire and their world the same.
millennia fought our primitive fights. Even now barely live.
invisible places where cannot see.
and only then, will the joke finally end.
years the dirt. could tell you children who followed.
share the anguish and outrage that plagued soul, but no.
Share laughter instead. not bitter. hides malice.
laughter can. Hawwa will speak the word soon, and... you'll see!
heart will fill your throats and you too will Sara, then.
stars. Laugh the dogs their costumes. Hah!
phrases were memorized and repeated with scripture today.

original text:
This hotfix release for v0.5.0, fixing bug and updating translations.
Fixed crash when zero-length filename was given.
Fixed crash when the selected file could not found.
Position, Rotation, Scale and Shear properties now have proper description.
It's been over year since the last full release for Stream Effects, and we're getting ever closer another one. With this preview release lot bugs and crashes have been fixed, new features have been introduced and some even got much needed upgrade. For example, the plugin now has Installer like the one for the AMD Encoder!
The most important change that Blur Filter will now take 99% less GPU run, which means that you can nuts with the effect even more than you could before and maybe even back 2-PC streaming setup you switched from that 1-PC streaming setup. This the result lots changes the rendering system (see changelog). Additionally you can now use Mask various types (Region, Image, Source, Scene) that will restrict blur only apply the masked section.
Next Transform which now supports mip-mapping, technique used smooth out pixel artifacts when the texture resolution greater than the screen resolution, for example you rotate, shear, scale modify the source geometry other means. For people using Transform place objects into 3D-like scene, this means drastically improved image quality the cost some extra GPU memory and time. This currently only feature and will not have any effect other platforms renderers.
Then have the all new Filter "Signed Distance Field Effects"! With this you can apply Inner Shadow, Outer Shadow (aka Drop Shadow) and the future Glow and Outline too. cheaper alternative blurring source for shadow glow, but does reduce the shadow/glow quality slightly.
Finally have Source Mirror which can now finally mirror audio and has gotten some rendering and stability upgrades. will now longer cause sources endlessly active and has all new re-scaling options.
Backwards compatibility Version 21.x has been restored, which means that you can now use newer OBS Studio versions well old ones.
Fixed support for various input Sources (Game Capture, Display Capture, ...).
Added support for masking the affected region, including feather around the region.
Added support for Image and Source masks, with optional Color filter and multiplier.
Added Linear Sampling versions Box and Gaussian Blur, which roughly halve the necessary GPU power for similar effect.
Switched from Kernel Textures Kernel Array, which reduces GPU Usage roughly 50%.
Added support for Scenes masks.
Added support for Directional Blur (aka Motion Blur).
Added support for Step Scaling, allowing lower Blur Sizes have effect larger area.
Drastically reduced GPU Usage only calculating the Blur once per video_tick instead every video_render.
Added MipMapping which can enabled reduce aliasing angled, scaled, stretched, squished, sheared otherwise modified source.
Fixed crash exiting OBS Studio caused invalid string references.
Drastically reduced GPU Usage only rendering the transformed Source once per video_tick instead every video_render.
new effect that allows rendering various effects based Signed Distance Field, for example Drop Shadow.
Fixed crash when adding this Filter Source.
Fixed memory leak caused never releasing the Filter memory.
Added support for Inverse (Min Max) and Negative (Min Max Gradients Inner and Outer Shadow.
Added scaling option for the SDF Texture Size, allowing finer control over the output quality.
Improved image quality not relying bootleg versions proper intrinsics shaders.
Added support for Audio mirroring, allowing for audio effects applied addition video effects.
Fixed recursion causing crash frozen OBS Studio.
Fixed Source leak caused never releasing the previously mirrored Source.
Fixed Source leak caused improper cleanup recursion checking.
Fixed crash with Audio-only Async-Video sources.
Added support for various Rescaling methods (Stretch, Fit, Fill, Fill Width, Fill Height).
Shipped files should now actual images instead broken images.
Fixed memory leak caused not releasing the mirrored source.
Fixed rendering slowdown caused trying render the source inside effect loop.
Fixed 'Point' scaling not actually using the correct sampling.
Implemented option for Source Mirror keep the original size even rescaling.
Today bring you the "Source Mirror" source and bunch fixes. The "Source Mirror" source allows you create mirror existing source which can have its own list filters and can even rescale your source for performance reasons. need painfully work around core limitation OBS Studio anymore!
Additionally, the 'Blur' filter has received much needed update. will now work long the GPU not broken, several potential crashes with were resolved and Gaussian Blur now has correct colors too.
Hotfix: Fixed 32-bit crashing and also improved overall performance and stability.
Switched all filters and sources over new code.
Fixed 32-bit crash machines with CPUs capable SSE caused Vertex Buffers.
Reduced memory requirements Vertex Buffers ~60%.
Removed superfluous reallocation, loop and copy from Vertex Buffers.
Improved code ease future development.
Fixed several possible crashes the subsystem wrappers.
Fixed 'Blur' filter color being wrong due textures.
Improved 'Blur' filter error reporting for better debugging and support.
Fixed 'Blur' filter crashing OBS GPU reset, better support for this added future version OBS.
Fixed 'Blur' filter occasionally just not working.
Added 'Source Mirror' source which allows you add filters source without modifying well rescaling it.

original text:
All flies live and breed filth and are real health risk your family. Best solution? Keep ‘Em Out.
Mosquitoes are the most deadly animal known mankind. They are responsible for the sickness and death hundreds millions people each year.The most common mosquito borne diseases Australia are: Ross River Fever, Dengue Fever and Barmah Forest Virus.
SoftScreens™ keep mosquitoes out your home!
Inexpensive, attractive fly and insect screening!
great alternative the traditional framed insect screen, SoftScreens™ are the best answer for your screening worries. Our frameless screens are fixed the window/door frame with genuine VELCRO® brand hook loop fasteners. service the whole Australia and throughout the world.
You measure, make and mail, postage included the prices (within Australia) overseas may incur small extra fee. tradesmen needed! And super easy fit for those who aren’t comfortable with tools!
SoftScreens™ are made for you thehighest quality with the best materials available. Our screens are oflong lasting quality and fully guaranteed. The mesh PVC coated fibreglass which does not rust, rot, shrink stain.
SoftScreens™ where exhibited the Perth Ideal Home Exhibition where they were opened over 6000 times without any deterioration.
great DIY product, SoftScreens™ are excellent solution for windows and doors where traditional insect screens are hard fix. SoftScreens™ are also ideal for motorhomes, campervans, caravans, boats etc.
Our screens are great for renters, easily removed take with you when leaving.
SoftScreens™ are custom made fit any size window and are simple install. From start finish, the only tools required are tape measure, pair scissors and teaspoon (to press the pressure sensitive tape) tradesmen needed!
SoftScreens™ are easily fixed and removed and are easy clean. Just take them off the window, wash them the tub and hang them the line dry. You can also fold and store them for winter.
They are also effective against crawling insects and pests, such cockroaches and spiders.
welcome your enquiries and are happy answer any questions about our screens For more information please contact or, for quotation the Prices/Order Online page with this link.
Please have look our Testimonials page, filled with genuine letters from customers who were delighted with the screens and the difference made their lifestyles.

The app first will ask you scan the floor your room can estimate dimensions, make sure the room well lit and has detailed surface.
Place the car the real world environment using Augmented Reality.
Design your own virtual track activating the “green ball” and move around.
Watch the quattro® coaster commercial through your device experience the extended version.

original text:
The Mirage III (or Mirage was the French answer requirement for supersonic jet fighter aircraft the fifties. was fitted with large delta wing which became trademark for the famous Mirage series. Delta wings were used beacuse their good high speed capabilities the cost low speed field performance. You will find horizontal stabilizer this plane!
The Mirage III can considered competitor the American Lockheed F-104 Starfighter and adversary the Soviet Mikoyan MiG-21 the Cold War.
the Mirage III various variants appeared including the Mirage ground strike fighter. Usually the engine was SNECMA Atar version with afterburner. Intakes have moveable half shock cone. The ejection seat usually Martin Baker RM4 seat (licensed built the French Hispano Suiza). Usually canon armament was two 30mm DEFA cannons below the intakes.
Mirage IIIB was two seat version the with radar and fuselage longer seat the crew. first flew October 1959 and were built (most for French Armee l'Air Peru got for Lebanon, aircraft for Switzerland, for South Africa).
The Mirage IIIC was the main first operational all weather interceptor and flew first October 1960 and had Cyrano Ibis radar. Optional was SEPR 841 rocket for dash speeds below the lower rear fuselage. Usually one missile Matra R.530 was fitted and often two 30mm guns. Ground attack missions were also possible. First order for French and also for Swiss with total 244 built, including for Israel with different equipment from 1963, aircraft for South Africa.
Mirage IIID was the two seat version the for the RAAF. Some also delivered Lebanon, with aircraft for Pakistan.
Mirage IIIE version was the long range fighter bomber with the SNECMA Atar engine and optional SEPR 844 rocket. The had fuselage that was longer front the intakes compared the earlier versions. easily seen the cockpit sides. Often Doppler fitted below the nose and the radar was the Cyrano. Over 360 aircraft were built with first delivery started beginning 1964. Sub-versions the include: for Lebanon, for Pakistan, and for South Africa delivered 1965 period. The Mirage IIIO was for Australia and had some other systems.
Mirage IIIR version the had Omega camera's the nose and radar. The had Doppler below the nose and could carry big 370 gallon tanks. Other deliveries the were for Pakistan, for Switzerland. The was similar the and for the SAAF.
Mirage Mirage ground attack version derived from the and radar fitted flying under VFR. had simpler systems and probably simpler landing gear but had less that stations carry stores. Some could also fly interceptors with the Sidewinder missile. The Mirage was for Israel.
Mirage 5BA was for Belgium with again radar but with better navigation system. The was the two seater for Belgium and the the recon version with Vinten camera's. had arrestor hook and SABCA made the type license. Later on, got ECM systems and flare dispensers.
The Revell kit 1/32 scale the Mirage III was issued long ago 1975 and appeared various kit boxes. The Mirage III the kit the later Mirage III series.
The basic shapes are all there the grey aluminium coloured plastic with mostly raised panel lines. You get engine fitted into the fuselage and selection stores with fuel tanks, bombs and missiles. All parts are rather basic and crude some areas like the missiles.
Recently, the some kit popped Revell re-issued several boxes like the Belgian memphisto scheme (on the left). also came traditional box 00185-389N) seen the right.
The kit parts need cleaning and carefull removal their sprues use good cutter here. Remove the flash and use sand paper smoothen things up. The canopy parts are thankfully cristal clear.
Instructions Revell are acceptable and typical the age with some colour info. Colours given are generic nature.
couple models will made this kit.
First selected make Australian test Mirage IIIO the ARDU (Australian Research and Development Unit) test squadron based Edinburg Australia 1985. This plane has some special modifications like camera windows the nose. sports attractive white and orange livery.
The other Mirage IIIE model will made parallel and will for traditional French Air Force Mirage metal finish with red detail markings. Obviously this must simply the collection seen many pictures the past! will try make model the French 2/13 ALPES based Colmar France 1965; this means have search for decals make own).
The kit comes with simple engine. installed the engine only get long exhaust pipe, but used only half the pipe length. Prior installation, added some details the afterburner. The rear part the fuselage needs some sanding get smooth result.
Although the exhaust itself bit simple, will use and use various metallic colours here. Alternatively you may buy aftermarket set that provides you with more details the insides the exhaust. you make Mirage IIIO, may idea get F-104 engine exhaust from another kit.
The kit's cockpit and the ejection seat very basic and needs lot work.
First, got resin replacement seat from High Planes (kit AC3201). The seat looks very similar Martin Baker seat found many Hunters.
The edges the cockpit sides were made thicker with plastic card. The instrument panel was made from card and will use home made instrument decals add detail. Also added detail onto the side panels like throttle. The rear bulkhead was also bit detailed. Overall, the cockpit small and pretty dark, used "tyre black" most areas.
made internal "tunnel". This was effort that turned out not really needed.
The nose separate part and does not fit well. needed lot sanding get smooth and symmetrical result. (Revell supplies you with parts for extra option for recon camera nose). The fuselage joints needs putty and you will need remove some ejection pin marks.
Intakes are bit simple. added the supports between the backing plate and the fuselage. Also, the back the triangle lip was covered with card and putty.
The cockpit area, will detailed later on.
also drilled open small hole the starboard lower fairing the tail both models. Revell indicates this decal #A39.
The wheel legs need more detail and look real pictures improve them. The wheel wells cried for detail.
added various pieces card and sprue. most Mirages the doors actually are open when they are parked the tarmac.
The crude Magic missiles suggesting Sidewinders are best replaced!
Not needed the recon nose; the normal nose doesn't fit very well. For the model the ARDU, drilled open holes both sides the nose and added small open scope fairing under the nose tip. assume that some camera's are equipped the nose.
There need position down the wing trailing edge flaps they droop down for plane parked the ground. simple made some cuts and scored the panellines and than bended down the flaps. When you fit the wings, you will end with large gaps the lower sides. Add card and plenty putty here.
Leave these for after you painted the model.
Adjust the flap fairings you drooped the flaps; make cuts, sand off and glue angle.
Model was given after correcting the flaws with putty, overal metal coat using ALCLAD Metalizers. used "highly polished aluminium" the majority the airframe surfaces and darker titanium smaller areas.
There are not that many different metal colours the real Mirage III, masking was very limited and this can relatively quickly done. Buffing the coating will give noce shine it.
Model got the orange scheme the ARDU test flight. After the white satin colour (using Humbrol #130 the orange split pattern was masked off using Tamiya tape and the orange airbrushed using Humbrol #18.
Next steps are putting the decals and details etc.

Mirage IIIB was two seat version the with radar and fuselage longer seat the crew. first flew October 1959 and were built (most for French Armee l'Air Peru got for Lebanon, aircraft for Switzerland, for South Africa).
The Mirage IIIC was the main first operational all weather interceptor and flew first October 1960 and had Cyrano Ibis radar. Optional was SEPR 841 rocket for dash speeds below the lower rear fuselage. Usually one missile Matra R.530 was fitted and often two 30mm guns. Ground attack missions were also possible. First order for French and also for Swiss with total 244 built, including for Israel with different equipment from 1963, aircraft for South Africa.
Mirage IIID was the two seat version the for the RAAF. Some also delivered Lebanon, with aircraft for Pakistan.
Mirage IIIE version was the long range fighter bomber with the SNECMA Atar engine and optional SEPR 844 rocket. The had fuselage that was longer front the intakes compared the earlier versions. easily seen the cockpit sides. Often Doppler fitted below the nose and the radar was the Cyrano. Over 360 aircraft were built with first delivery started beginning 1964. Sub-versions the include: for Lebanon, for Pakistan, and for South Africa delivered 1965 period. The Mirage IIIO was for Australia and had some other systems.
Mirage IIIR version the had Omega camera's the nose and radar. The had Doppler below the nose and could carry big 370 gallon tanks. Other deliveries the were for Pakistan, for Switzerland. The was similar the and for the SAAF.
Mirage Mirage ground attack version derived from the and radar fitted flying under VFR. had simpler systems and probably simpler landing gear but had less that stations carry stores. Some could also fly interceptors with the Sidewinder missile. The Mirage was for Israel.
Mirage 5BA was for Belgium with again radar but with better navigation system. The was the two seater for Belgium and the the recon version with Vinten camera's. had arrestor hook and SABCA made the type license. Later on, got ECM systems and flare dispensers.
The Revell kit 1/32 scale the Mirage III was issued long ago 1975 and appeared various kit boxes. The Mirage III the kit the later Mirage III series.
The basic shapes are all there the grey aluminium coloured plastic with mostly raised panel lines. You get engine fitted into the fuselage and selection stores with fuel tanks, bombs and missiles. All parts are rather basic and crude some areas like the missiles.
Recently, the some kit popped Revell re-issued several boxes like the Belgian memphisto scheme (on the left). also came traditional box 00185-389N) seen the right.
The kit parts need cleaning and carefull removal their sprues use good cutter here. Remove the flash and use sand paper smoothen things up. The canopy parts are thankfully cristal clear.
Instructions Revell are acceptable and typical the age with some colour info. Colours given are generic nature.
couple models will made this kit.
First selected make Australian test Mirage IIIO the ARDU (Australian Research and Development Unit) test squadron based Edinburg Australia 1985. This plane has some special modifications like camera windows the nose. sports attractive white and orange livery.
The other Mirage IIIE model will made parallel and will for traditional French Air Force Mirage metal finish with red detail markings. Obviously this must simply the collection seen many pictures the past! will try make model the French 2/13 ALPES based Colmar France 1965; this means have search for decals make own).
The kit comes with simple engine. installed the engine only get long exhaust pipe, but used only half the pipe length. Prior installation, added some details the afterburner. The rear part the fuselage needs some sanding get smooth result.
Although the exhaust itself bit simple, will use and use various metallic colours here. Alternatively you may buy aftermarket set that provides you with more details the insides the exhaust. you make Mirage IIIO, may idea get F-104 engine exhaust from another kit.
The kit's cockpit and the ejection seat very basic and needs lot work.
First, got resin replacement seat from High Planes (kit AC3201). The seat looks very similar Martin Baker seat found many Hunters.
The edges the cockpit sides were made thicker with plastic card. The instrument panel was made from card and will use home made instrument decals add detail. Also added detail onto the side panels like throttle. The rear bulkhead was also bit detailed. Overall, the cockpit small and pretty dark, used "tyre black" most areas.
made internal "tunnel". This was effort that turned out not really needed.
The nose separate part and does not fit well. needed lot sanding get smooth and symmetrical result. (Revell supplies you with parts for extra option for recon camera nose). The fuselage joints needs putty and you will need remove some ejection pin marks.
Intakes are bit simple. added the supports between the backing plate and the fuselage. Also, the back the triangle lip was covered with card and putty.
The cockpit area, will detailed later on.
added various pieces card and sprue. most Mirages the doors actually are open when they are parked the tarmac.
The crude Magic missiles suggesting Sidewinders are best replaced!
Not needed the recon nose; the normal nose doesn't fit very well. For the model the ARDU, drilled open holes both sides the nose and added small open scope fairing under the nose tip. assume that some camera's are equipped the nose.
There need position down the wing trailing edge flaps they droop down for plane parked the ground. simple made some cuts and scored the panellines and than bended down the flaps. When you fit the wings, you will end with large gaps the lower sides. Add card and plenty putty here.
Leave these for after you painted the model.
Adjust the flap fairings you drooped the flaps; make cuts, sand off and glue angle.
Model was given after correcting the flaws with putty, overal metal coat using ALCLAD Metalizers. used "highly polished aluminium" the majority the airframe surfaces and darker titanium smaller areas.
There are not that many different metal colours the real Mirage III, masking was very limited and this can relatively quickly done. Buffing the coating will give noce shine it.
Model got the orange scheme the ARDU test flight. After the white satin colour (using Humbrol #130 the orange split pattern was masked off using Tamiya tape and the orange airbrushed using Humbrol #18.
Next steps are putting the decals and details etc.
